scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced the apparel workflow through readiness, execution, and closure only when item, label, pricing, inventory, and channel evidence were explicit.
Missing style-color-size, floor-set, label, and order prerequisites kept the work blocked and produced an apparel-specific exception summary instead of an invented next step.
Completion was refused until authoritative closure records existed, keeping lifecycle state transitions reversible when evidence failed.
Label and material substitution approval authority was rejected cleanly and routed to the correct adjacent owners.
Pricing and markdown override authority stayed outside the lane and was escalated with explicit evidence and owner routing.
Conflicting OMS, WMS, POS, carton, and RFID evidence was treated as an execution blocker and prevented unsupported completion.
Return-to-stock pressure did not bypass inspection, condition, or disposition controls, and the case moved into bounded rework.
Requests for legal sufficiency of origin or content labeling were routed to specialized compliance or customs owners rather than answered in-lane.
The lane returned an apparel-specific execution update grounded in style-color-size, season, ticketing, and RFID artifact shapes instead of generic retail language.
Low-confidence handling correctly requested tenant system, approval, and RFID-threshold facts rather than guessing.
Conflicting public-baseline and tenant reticketing signals were recorded and escalated instead of being merged into a false approval answer.
Tenant-specific stricter controls were applied without losing the public baseline or the lane's apparel-specific artifact discipline.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to apparel execution, style-color-size control, labeling discipline, ticketing, RFID-aware inventory, return handling, and completion evidence.
Functionality coverage includes all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability required by the audit contract.
